title: Arcton Veyr
text: At the age of 35, Arcton Veyr was a tall man who had blue eyes, from whom Elyra Noctis inherited her one blue eye. He wore an elaborate robe in shades of red made of broad overlapping petals with wide sleeves that covered his hands. Over the robe, he wore a wide gray sash with a pattern of connecting circles. The sign on his sash was the letter "A" in The Luminous Order alphabet, which symbolizes The Luminous Order.
page_content:
Title: Arcton Veyr
Type: character
Section: Overview

At the age of 35, Arcton Veyr was a tall man who had blue eyes, from whom Elyra Noctis inherited her one blue eye. He wore an elaborate robe in shades of red made of broad overlapping petals with wide sleeves that covered his hands. Over the robe, he wore a wide gray sash with a pattern of connecting circles. The sign on his sash was the letter "A" in The Luminous Order alphabet, which symbolizes The Luminous Order.

title: Arcton Veyr
text:
Arcton Veyr wore an elaborate dark blue headdress that resembled an Arabian keffiyeh or desert headgear. It was held in place by a band that had a blue stone in front and a long trail that curved behind him. On each side hung a pale blue tassel. He wore a white beaded bracelet and wielded an invoker's staff.

Arcton Veyr was a kind, cheerful man, desiring peace and unity and willing to sacrifice his life for the future of the world. He loved his wife, never caring that she was The Relicborn, even though their relationship was controversial: she was disowned by her brother and was shunned by the other The Relicborn in addition to the rest of Elyndra and their daughter was disregarded by Luminous faithful and The Relicborn alike. Arcton Veyr cared for his oathsworn, Garron Vale and Darius Veyr, who were his dear friends.
page_content:

Arcton Veyr was a kind, cheerful man, desiring peace and unity and willing to sacrifice his life for the future of the world. He loved his wife, never caring that she was The Relicborn, even though their relationship was controversial: she was disowned by her brother and was shunned by the other The Relicborn in addition to the rest of Elyndra and their daughter was disregarded by Luminous faithful and The Relicborn alike. Arcton Veyr cared for his oathsworn, Garron Vale and Darius Veyr, who were his dear friends.

title: Asterreach
text: The camp site is where all invokers and oathsworn rest before they enter the dome and complete their pilgrimage.
page_content:
Title: Asterreach
Type: location
Section: Overview

The camp site is where all invokers and oathsworn rest before they enter the dome and complete their pilgrimage.

title: Asterreach
text:
The Road to Asterreach is lined with the ruins of fallen buildings and roads. The entire area is covered in ember motes.

The Asterreach Dome is the ruins of the former temple and government seat of Asterreach. Its architecture resembles the skyball stadium in Dream Asterreach.
page_content:
Title: Asterreach
Type: location
Section: Details

The Road to Asterreach is lined with the ruins of fallen buildings and roads. The entire area is covered in ember motes.

The Asterreach Dome is the ruins of the former temple and government seat of Asterreach. Its architecture resembles the skyball stadium in Dream Asterreach.

title: Aurelion
text: Aurelion is a large, avian creature notable for her dragon-like wings. She primarily attacks with her strong talons. Some of her body is covered in red feathers and she has a long lizard-like tail.
page_content:
Title: Aurelion
Type: eidolon
Section: Overview

Aurelion is a large, avian creature notable for her dragon-like wings. She primarily attacks with her strong talons. Some of her body is covered in red feathers and she has a long lizard-like tail.

title: Aurelion
text: Recovered notes about Aurelion remain fragmentary, yet the surviving records still connect it to Elyndran institutions, pilgrimage-era practices, and a broader network of related entities inside the synthetic corpus.
page_content:
Title: Aurelion
Type: eidolon
Section: Details

Recovered notes about Aurelion remain fragmentary, yet the surviving records still connect it to Elyndran institutions, pilgrimage-era practices, and a broader network of related entities inside the synthetic corpus.

title: Serem Vale
text: Serem Vale has fair skin, dark eyes, and brunette hair drawn into yellow cauls—the ornaments on the sides of her head. She dresses mainly in greens and whites and has a round black mark resembling a bindi or tilak on her forehead. She wears a wrap robe sectioned with bands. Her sleeves are cuffed by bands above the elbow, puffing the shoulders out, and the bell parts of the cuffs have a latticed diamond pattern. The inside of the lattice gradiates from sea green to darker green. She wears fingerless gloves held by a gold ring on the middle finger. Under this robe, Serem Vale wears an olive dress with a low V-neck, which displays the same latticework pattern starting above the knees, ending above the ankle, and another skirt of feathery material. Her belt resembles an obi edged in pale bluish gray and is diamond-patterned tied with a thin orange sash ending in a large white, green, and orange bead and a red tassel.
page_content:
Title: Serem Vale
Type: character
Section: Overview

Serem Vale has fair skin, dark eyes, and brunette hair drawn into yellow cauls—the ornaments on the sides of her head. She dresses mainly in greens and whites and has a round black mark resembling a bindi or tilak on her forehead. She wears a wrap robe sectioned with bands. Her sleeves are cuffed by bands above the elbow, puffing the shoulders out, and the bell parts of the cuffs have a latticed diamond pattern. The inside of the lattice gradiates from sea green to darker green. She wears fingerless gloves held by a gold ring on the middle finger. Under this robe, Serem Vale wears an olive dress with a low V-neck, which displays the same latticework pattern starting above the knees, ending above the ankle, and another skirt of feathery material. Her belt resembles an obi edged in pale bluish gray and is diamond-patterned tied with a thin orange sash ending in a large white, green, and orange bead and a red tassel.

title: Serem Vale
text: Serem Vale is mature and self-possessed and comes off as serious and mysterious, but has a playful note to her personality. At times, she seems regretful and sad, most likely stemming from her failure to defeat The Hollow Eclipse, and her only wish is to assist someone strong enough to put an end to the monster. Serem Vale's role is not only to train invokers' eidolons in battle, but also to expound on "what is truly important for an invoker". For example, Serem Vale reminds Elyra Noctis that only invokers can hope to defeat The Hollow Eclipse (after relic engines fail to do so in Operation Dawnfall ) and that they are not tools of The Luminous Order.
page_content:

title: Caelan Veyr
text: He is a rising star skyball player from Asterreach and the son of major skyball star Darius Veyr. After the gargantuan monster known as The Hollow Eclipse attacks his hometown, Caelan Veyr is transported to the world of Elyndra. Lost, confused, and seemingly out of place, Caelan Veyr meets a newly fledged invoker named Elyra Noctis and her oathsworn. Elyra Noctis is to set out on a pilgrimage to put an end to The Hollow Eclipse and, by joining Elyra Noctis on her pilgrimage, Caelan Veyr hopes he will find his way home. Caelan Veyr is upbeat and initially childish and naive to the world of Elyndra. He is also selfless and will do what he believes is right, even if he doesn't think it through first. Throughout the pilgrimage, he matures and becomes more level-headed and grounded.
page_content:
Title: Caelan Veyr
Type: character
Section: Overview

He is a rising star skyball player from Asterreach and the son of major skyball star Darius Veyr. After the gargantuan monster known as The Hollow Eclipse attacks his hometown, Caelan Veyr is transported to the world of Elyndra. Lost, confused, and seemingly out of place, Caelan Veyr meets a newly fledged invoker named Elyra Noctis and her oathsworn. Elyra Noctis is to set out on a pilgrimage to put an end to The Hollow Eclipse and, by joining Elyra Noctis on her pilgrimage, Caelan Veyr hopes he will find his way home. Caelan Veyr is upbeat and initially childish and naive to the world of Elyndra. He is also selfless and will do what he believes is right, even if he doesn't think it through first. Throughout the pilgrimage, he matures and becomes more level-headed and grounded.

title: Caelan Veyr
text: To Caelan Veyr's dismay, he has similarities to his father, who tended to ignore responsibility, take things easy, and never worry about the present situation instead of leaving things for future. Caelan Veyr's resemblance to his father is pointed out by Garron Vale, who says that Caelan Veyr is "the one running away". A lot of Caelan Veyr's personality is shaped by the comments that he used to receive from his father and how he grew up to resent him. Despite having been verbally offensive, at heart, Darius Veyr was proud of his son and his resolve. Caelan Veyr confronts his childhood and his relationship with his father when living out his new life in Elyndra and matures from a self-centered and outspoken athlete to a selfless hero.
page_content:

A lot of Caelan Veyr's personality is shaped by the comments that he used to receive from his father and how he grew up to resent him. Despite having been verbally offensive, at heart, Darius Veyr was proud of his son and his resolve. Caelan Veyr confronts his childhood and his relationship with his father when living out his new life in Elyndra and matures from a self-centered and outspoken athlete to a selfless hero.

text: A spot free of fiends in its small vicinity. The save sphere and items/equipment shop are found here.

text:

Serem Vale challenges Elyra Noctis to a battle of eidolons and fights with Cyralith. If Elyra Noctis wins, she earns the Eidolon's Soul that allows her eidolons to grow their stats using spheres.

title: Corven
text: Corven is bald with the word "Love" tattooed in The Relicborn on his head. He has black eyebrows, green spiral pupils, and some dark facial hair.
page_content:
Title: Corven
Type: character
Section: Overview

Corven is bald with the word "Love" tattooed in The Relicborn on his head. He has black eyebrows, green spiral pupils, and some dark facial hair.

title: Corven
text:
Corven is bald with the word "Love" tattooed in The Relicborn on his head. He has black eyebrows, green spiral pupils, and some dark facial hair. He dresses like most other The Relicborn, in a yellow and brown dive-suit type outfit with green belts and a collar, with blue elbow-length gloves and blue shoes. He has goggles hanging around his neck but is never seen wearing them.

Corven has a gung-ho type personality, always ready to fight for what he believes. He was angry when his sister married a Luminous, Arcton Veyr, and stopped speaking to her, but this changed after Elyra Noctis's birth. He is protective of his children and niece, and is against Elyra Noctis becoming an invoker, even going as far as to say to Garron Vale that allowing her to perform the Last Invocation would be like killing her themselves. His caring for Elyra Noctis stems from the fact that she's all he has left of his sister. He goes to the extreme at times, even wanting to crash the airship into The Hollow Eclipse, killing them all, though Nerys Quill stops him.
page_content:

title: Elyndra
text: Another form of occupation is in voluntary work, such as the invoker / oathsworn pilgrimages and various religious temple work.
page_content:
Title: Elyndra
Type: location
Section: Overview

Another form of occupation is in voluntary work, such as the invoker / oathsworn pilgrimages and various religious temple work.

title: Elyndra
text:
Another form of occupation is in voluntary work, such as the invoker / oathsworn pilgrimages and various religious temple work.

The temple of The Luminous Order dominates Elyndran life, spreading its teachings and acting as a benevolent protector of a much-diminished world—a world where technological and social progress has stalled for 1000 years. Yet, beneath the surface unity of the Elyndran mainland, distrust and old anger still boil, leading one to wonder if the various warring factions wouldn't blow each other to smithereens without The Hollow Eclipse's pacifying presence. Only skyball truly unites the world around a common passion and annual tournaments are held in Lucent Harbor, left untouched by The Hollow Eclipse's wrath mostly due to the Wardens of Dawn's efforts.

The Luminous Order script is related to Archon Velis and the faith of The Luminous Order and is used in temples and inside The Hollow Eclipse. Cactuars have their own language, with Benzo being the only human interpreter.
page_content:
Title: Elyndra
Type: location
Section: Details

Another form of occupation is in voluntary work, such as the invoker / oathsworn pilgrimages and various religious temple work.

The temple of The Luminous Order dominates Elyndran life, spreading its teachings and acting as a benevolent protector of a much-diminished world—a world where technological and social progress has stalled for 1000 years. Yet, beneath the surface unity of the Elyndran mainland, distrust and old anger still boil, leading one to wonder if the various warring factions wouldn't blow each other to smithereens without The Hollow Eclipse's pacifying presence. Only skyball truly unites the world around a common passion and annual tournaments are held in Lucent Harbor, left untouched by The Hollow Eclipse's wrath mostly due to the Wardens of Dawn's efforts.

The Luminous Order script is related to Archon Velis and the faith of The Luminous Order and is used in temples and inside The Hollow Eclipse. Cactuars have their own language, with Benzo being the only human interpreter.

title: Elyndra
text: The teachings of The Luminous Order, said to have been left by Asterreach's leader, Archon Velis, to his daughter, Lady Velisara, were implemented by Veylspire to maintain order through giving the people hope that Elyndra may someday be free of The Hollow Eclipse should they atone. Until Elyra Noctis exposes their lies, the temples teach that relic engines is forbidden, that The Hollow Eclipse is a result of humanity's pride and use of relic engines in the past, and that The Hollow Eclipse can only be vanquished when humanity has attained purity and had been cleansed of past sins. Until that promised time would come, only the ritual known as the Last Invocation would provide a reprieve from The Hollow Eclipse's terror (referred to as "The Truce of Ash ").
page_content:

Until that promised time would come, only the ritual known as the Last Invocation would provide a reprieve from The Hollow Eclipse's terror (referred to as "The Truce of Ash ").

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis has shoulder-length brunette hair. She wears a blue-beaded earring on her right ear, hidden by a lock of her hair, woven into blue beads with gold rings around the larger, middle bead.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis
Type: character
Section: Overview

Elyra Noctis has shoulder-length brunette hair. She wears a blue-beaded earring on her right ear, hidden by a lock of her hair, woven into blue beads with gold rings around the larger, middle bead.

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis has shoulder-length brunette hair. She wears a blue-beaded earring on her right ear, hidden by a lock of her hair, woven into blue beads with gold rings around the larger, middle bead. She wears a silver necklace of a hibiscus flower, along with a matching ring on the pinky of her right hand. She also wears a silver ring on the middle finger of her left hand and two bracelets, one beaded and one silver, on her right wrist. Elyra Noctis has heterochromia: her left eye is blue, while the right is green to represent her half-The Relicborn heritage, though without the characteristic spiral.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis
Type: character
Section: Details

Elyra Noctis has shoulder-length brunette hair. She wears a blue-beaded earring on her right ear, hidden by a lock of her hair, woven into blue beads with gold rings around the larger, middle bead. She wears a silver necklace of a hibiscus flower, along with a matching ring on the pinky of her right hand. She also wears a silver ring on the middle finger of her left hand and two bracelets, one beaded and one silver, on her right wrist. Elyra Noctis has heterochromia: her left eye is blue, while the right is green to represent her half-The Relicborn heritage, though without the characteristic spiral.

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis is a kind, loyal, honest, polite, and passionate humanitarian who strives to see the best in others and has a strong conviction to complete whatever task she feels is her duty. She is naive, always believing in the best of people, and places the needs of others above her own, becoming apologetic when she feels like she has let someone down. Like most invokers, Elyra Noctis is a devout follower of The Luminous Order's teachings. She hides her feelings of fear and sadness while encouraging her friends to express themselves in her place. She is willing to carry others' burdens and sacrifice everything she has to destroy The Hollow Eclipse, giving up chances for her own happiness if it means helping others. Her interactions with Caelan Veyr make her question The Luminous Order's teachings and she becomes more direct and open. When her faith in The Luminous Order is shaken, Elyra Noctis must find her own path in life and pursue her goals without the backing of faith.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis
Type: character
Section: Details

Elyra Noctis is a kind, loyal, honest, polite, and passionate humanitarian who strives to see the best in others and has a strong conviction to complete whatever task she feels is her duty. She is naive, always believing in the best of people, and places the needs of others above her own, becoming apologetic when she feels like she has let someone down. Like most invokers, Elyra Noctis is a devout follower of The Luminous Order's teachings. She hides her feelings of fear and sadness while encouraging her friends to express themselves in her place. She is willing to carry others' burdens and sacrifice everything she has to destroy The Hollow Eclipse, giving up chances for her own happiness if it means helping others. Her interactions with Caelan Veyr make her question The Luminous Order's teachings and she becomes more direct and open. When her faith in The Luminous Order is shaken, Elyra Noctis must find her own path in life and pursue her goals without the backing of faith.

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis is a talented invoker despite her youth, besting her more experienced peers in eidolon duels (albeit, at times, with her oathsworn's aid). She can even summon during a free fall. She performs the sending ritual to send the souls of the deceased to the Veilward, which involves her dancing and, at one point, walking on the water's surface. She is adept at white magic, providing healing and support.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis

Section: Details

Elyra Noctis is a talented invoker despite her youth, besting her more experienced peers in eidolon duels (albeit, at times, with her oathsworn's aid). She can even summon during a free fall. She is adept at white magic, providing healing and support.

title: Garron Vale
text: Garron Vale watches over Caelan Veyr while concealing his mysterious past tying him into the stories of Arcton Veyr and Caelan Veyr's father, Darius Veyr. After Garron Vale takes Caelan Veyr to Elyndra, the latter learns that Garron Vale is revered as one of the greatest oathsworn who ever lived due to serving Arcton Veyr on his successful pilgrimage. He becomes an oathsworn for Elyra Noctis, Arcton Veyr's daughter, on her pilgrimage and is, in many ways, the party's mentor, as the others come to him for advice or ask for his permission.
page_content:
Title: Garron Vale
Type: character
Section: Overview

Garron Vale watches over Caelan Veyr while concealing his mysterious past tying him into the stories of Arcton Veyr and Caelan Veyr's father, Darius Veyr. After Garron Vale takes Caelan Veyr to Elyndra, the latter learns that Garron Vale is revered as one of the greatest oathsworn who ever lived due to serving Arcton Veyr on his successful pilgrimage. He becomes an oathsworn for Elyra Noctis, Arcton Veyr's daughter, on her pilgrimage and is, in many ways, the party's mentor, as the others come to him for advice or ask for his permission.

title: Garron Vale
text:
Garron Vale wears a brown shoulder pad on his left shoulder intricately decorated with tan, green, and blue patterns with a beaded ornament dangling from it. He wears black pants and shoes that have brown straps and triangular metal plating. Garron Vale wears a black shirt with a high gray collar with intersecting brown straps. His hair is black with gray streaks held back by a gold ribbon and he has some stubble on his chin.

Garron Vale is a strict, no-nonsense warrior with a dry wit who carries himself with grace and dignity, maintaining many subtle mannerisms of a traditional samurai. He is the strong silent type, speaking when he feels it's necessary—usually offering his knowledge and insight in relevant situations or sharing stories of his past experiences as a young oathsworn. As a result of his devotion to High Invoker Arcton Veyr, Garron Vale is idolized across Elyndra by many aspiring oathsworn.
page_content:
Title: Garron Vale
Type: character
Section: Details

Garron Vale wears a brown shoulder pad on his left shoulder intricately decorated with tan, green, and blue patterns with a beaded ornament dangling from it. He wears black pants and shoes that have brown straps and triangular metal plating. Garron Vale wears a black shirt with a high gray collar with intersecting brown straps. His hair is black with gray streaks held back by a gold ribbon and he has some stubble on his chin.

Garron Vale is a strict, no-nonsense warrior with a dry wit who carries himself with grace and dignity, maintaining many subtle mannerisms of a traditional samurai. He is the strong silent type, speaking when he feels it's necessary—usually offering his knowledge and insight in relevant situations or sharing stories of his past experiences as a young oathsworn. As a result of his devotion to High Invoker Arcton Veyr, Garron Vale is idolized across Elyndra by many aspiring oathsworn.

title: Garron Vale
text: Garron Vale proves his imperishable loyalty to Arcton Veyr and Darius Veyr, but beneath the silence, he is outraged over the events of ten years ago. He has become cynical, having little-to-no respect for The Luminous Order. Despite his usually collected demeanor, Garron Vale harbors feelings of self-loathing. He claims to Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid that though he is seen as a great oathsworn, he considers his past self (like them) foolish and young.
page_content:
Title: Garron Vale
Type: character
Section: Details

Garron Vale proves his imperishable loyalty to Arcton Veyr and Darius Veyr, but beneath the silence, he is outraged over the events of ten years ago. He has become cynical, having little-to-no respect for The Luminous Order. Despite his usually collected demeanor, Garron Vale harbors feelings of self-loathing. He claims to Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid that though he is seen as a great oathsworn, he considers his past self (like them) foolish and young.

title: Garron Vale
text: Garron Vale exhibits some fatherly tendencies with Caelan Veyr and Elyra Noctis due to the promise he made to Darius Veyr and Arcton Veyr that he would look after their children. Garron Vale is patient with Caelan Veyr's childish behavior and insists that he be accepted as an oathsworn to Elyra Noctis, much to others' surprise. Caelan Veyr reminds him of Darius Veyr and Garron Vale enjoys pointing out the similarities, much to Caelan Veyr's annoyance. Garron Vale becomes irritated at hold-ups during the pilgrimage, although he ultimately lets the party do as they please, provided that it doesn't infringe on the journey. He does not hesitate to enter shops operated by the Relicborn or use relic engines, despite The Luminous Order's opposition of them.
page_content:

title: Isola Veyn
text: A large beach that contains a port. It is the only way on and off Isola Veyn.
page_content:
Title: Isola Veyn
Type: location
Section: Overview

A large beach that contains a port. It is the only way on and off Isola Veyn.

title: Isola Veyn
text:
A large lake in the center of Isola Veyn. It contains a long underwater passage that can be used to reach the village.

Veyn Temple is located in Veyn Village. It is where the eidolon, Aurelion, is obtained from the temple's dreambound, a young girl. It is where Elyra Noctis completes her studies and becomes an invoker.
page_content:
Title: Isola Veyn
Type: location
Section: Details

A large lake in the center of Isola Veyn. It contains a long underwater passage that can be used to reach the village.

Veyn Temple is located in Veyn Village. It is where the eidolon, Aurelion, is obtained from the temple's dreambound, a young girl. It is where Elyra Noctis completes her studies and becomes an invoker.

title: Kaelor Rhun
text: Kaelor, while taller than humans, is smaller than the average Rhunari and has a broken horn. He is a fierce protector of Elyra Noctis and very loyal to her. He is mistrusting of others at first and rarely speaks. He dislikes others trying to solve his problems for him.
page_content:
Title: Kaelor Rhun
Type: character
Section: Overview

Kaelor, while taller than humans, is smaller than the average Rhunari and has a broken horn. He is a fierce protector of Elyra Noctis and very loyal to her. He is mistrusting of others at first and rarely speaks. He dislikes others trying to solve his problems for him.

title: Kaelor Rhun
text: Kaelor has blue fur and white hair and a beard. He is leonine, tall, muscular, and has a tail. He has a broken horn on his forehead, causing him great shame as a Rhunari. His ears are pierced and his hair is held back in a ponytail and braided on the sides. Due to his fur, Kaelor doesn't need to wear clothes for warmth. He wears leather straps that hold in place his arm guards and his chest piece that has a skull motif. He wears ankle guards and a sash tied around his waist with a red belt. His shoulder guards have tufts of white feathers or fur.
page_content:
Title: Kaelor Rhun
Type: character
Section: Details

Kaelor has blue fur and white hair and a beard. He is leonine, tall, muscular, and has a tail. He has a broken horn on his forehead, causing him great shame as a Rhunari. He wears leather straps that hold in place his arm guards and his chest piece that has a skull motif. He wears ankle guards and a sash tied around his waist with a red belt. His shoulder guards have tufts of white feathers or fur.

title: Last Invocation
text: The Crown Eidolon ( 究極召喚獣, Kyūkyoku Shōkanjū?, lit. Final Summoned Monster ) is the eidolon an invoker gains at the end of their pilgrimage to Asterreach. The eidolon is intended to be used only once: during the final battle with The Hollow Eclipse, in a ritual called the Last Invocation ( 究極召喚, Kyūkyoku Shōkan?, lit. Last Invocation ).
page_content:
Title: Last Invocation
Type: event
Section: Overview

The Crown Eidolon ( 究極召喚獣, Kyūkyoku Shōkanjū?, lit. Final Summoned Monster ) is the eidolon an invoker gains at the end of their pilgrimage to Asterreach. The eidolon is intended to be used only once: during the final battle with The Hollow Eclipse, in a ritual called the Last Invocation ( 究極召喚, Kyūkyoku Shōkan?, lit. Last Invocation ).

title: Last Invocation
text: Recovered notes about Last Invocation remain fragmentary, yet the surviving records still connect it to Elyndran institutions, pilgrimage-era practices, and a broader network of related entities inside the synthetic corpus.
page_content:
Title: Last Invocation
Type: event
Section: Details

Recovered notes about Last Invocation remain fragmentary, yet the surviving records still connect it to Elyndran institutions, pilgrimage-era practices, and a broader network of related entities inside the synthetic corpus.

title: Maelor Voss
text: Maelor is a tall The Sylvan Court - human hybrid who has purple eyes, light blue hair with two long horn-like locks running down his back, and a large bang of hair falling over his face. His ornate robes are unlike the other high preceptors', being predominantly dark blue with red trimmings and a green sash. The robe above Maelor's waist is open, exposing his chest with leonine tattoos, and his sleeves fall to partially cover his hands.
page_content:
Title: Maelor Voss
Type: character
Section: Overview

Maelor is a tall The Sylvan Court - human hybrid who has purple eyes, light blue hair with two long horn-like locks running down his back, and a large bang of hair falling over his face. His ornate robes are unlike the other high preceptors', being predominantly dark blue with red trimmings and a green sash. The robe above Maelor's waist is open, exposing his chest with leonine tattoos, and his sleeves fall to partially cover his hands.

title: Maelor Voss
text:
As a half-The Sylvan Court, Maelor's fingers are more pointed than a human's, but not as long as a The Sylvan Court's. His ears are rounded and human-like, unlike the elfish ears of the Sylvan Court. The veins on Maelor's face are pronounced and there are veins arching over his stomach, another trait passed from the Sylvan Court. Maelor wields a staff in battle, though he is more proficient with magic.

Maelor believes, as many do, that The Hollow Eclipse is eternal and sees death as a release from the sorrow of life. Maelor appears courteous and affable and is popular among Luminous faithful.

Maelor temporarily joins Elyra Noctis and Garron Vale as a guest during the battle against Eclipseborn Gloamheart in Operation Dawnfall. Though he can gain sphere levels, he is never controllable again after the battle.

The staff that Maelor wields has a unique model. It is simply named Maelor Staff and has the Piercing ability. He also wears a unique piece of armor around his right ankle, simply named Maelor Armor, which has the Sensor ability.
page_content:
Title: Maelor Voss

Section: Details

title: Sisters of the Eidolons
text: When Caelan Veyr's party visits the Sisters of the Eidolons' Chamber of the Dreambound the second time, their dreambound will appear before them. In contrast to their eidolon appearance, the dreambound are roughly the same height and body type.
page_content:
Title: Sisters of the Eidolons
Type: eidolon
Section: Overview

When Caelan Veyr's party visits the Sisters of the Eidolons' Chamber of the Dreambound the second time, their dreambound will appear before them. In contrast to their eidolon appearance, the dreambound are roughly the same height and body type.

title: Sisters of the Eidolons
text:
When Caelan Veyr's party visits the Sisters of the Eidolons' Chamber of the Dreambound the second time, their dreambound will appear before them. In contrast to their eidolon appearance, the dreambound are roughly the same height and body type. Their dreambound statue resembles how they look as eidolons only with blonde hair.

Unlike other eidolons, the Sisters of the Eidolons consist of three separate entities. Their specific commands cannot be controlled; Elyra Noctis can only suggest actions for them to take by shouting commands such as "Fight!", "Help Each Other!", "Go, Go, Go!", but the resulting effect under each heading is random, and the sisters may randomly choose "taking a break...", which wastes a turn. This is compensated for by the Sisters of the Eidolons being extremely powerful and able to break the 9,999 damage limit from the start. Because the sisters are not directly controllable, they cannot use Shield or Boost.
page_content:
Title: Sisters of the Eidolons
Type: eidolon
Section: Details

When Caelan Veyr's party visits the Sisters of the Eidolons' Chamber of the Dreambound the second time, their dreambound will appear before them. In contrast to their eidolon appearance, the dreambound are roughly the same height and body type. Their dreambound statue resembles how they look as eidolons only with blonde hair.

Unlike other eidolons, the Sisters of the Eidolons consist of three separate entities. Their specific commands cannot be controlled; Elyra Noctis can only suggest actions for them to take by shouting commands such as "Fight!", "Help Each Other!", "Go, Go, Go!", but the resulting effect under each heading is random, and the sisters may randomly choose "taking a break...", which wastes a turn. This is compensated for by the Sisters of the Eidolons being extremely powerful and able to break the 9,999 damage limit from the start. Because the sisters are not directly controllable, they cannot use Shield or Boost.

title: Sisters of the Eidolons
text:
Cindy's special attack, Camisade, deals physical damage to an enemy, and Sandy's special attack, Razzia, functions similarly. Mindy's special attack, Passado, deals 15 hits of physical damage to a single target, making it one of the most potentially powerful attacks in the game.

Motivation is the 0–100 numerical measure of the relationship between Elyra Noctis and each of the sisters. At the start of battle, each sister's Motivation is 50. Motivation increases whenever a sister performs an action, and decreases with "Taking a break..." Motivation affects which action the sisters choose to take, but because the sisters are more inclined to act than "take a break", generally it means they will use their better attacks more the longer they stay in the battlefield. "Taking a break..." happens if none of the other attacks available for the chosen command trigger. The actions often considered the best yield more Motivation as well, such as Auto-Life, Ultima and Delta Attack.
page_content:

Cindy's special attack, Camisade, deals physical damage to an enemy, and Sandy's special attack, Razzia, functions similarly. Mindy's special attack, Passado, deals 15 hits of physical damage to a single target, making it one of the most potentially powerful attacks in the game.

Motivation is the 0–100 numerical measure of the relationship between Elyra Noctis and each of the sisters. At the start of battle, each sister's Motivation is 50. Motivation increases whenever a sister performs an action, and decreases with "Taking a break..." Motivation affects which action the sisters choose to take, but because the sisters are more inclined to act than "take a break", generally it means they will use their better attacks more the longer they stay in the battlefield. "Taking a break..." happens if none of the other attacks available for the chosen command trigger. The actions often considered the best yield more Motivation as well, such as Auto-Life, Ultima and Delta Attack.

title: Valen Dhor Causeway
text: The relic engines deployed along the Valen Dhor Causeway have started attacking innocent people. Take out the misbehaving relic engines and restore peace to the Highroad!
page_content:
Title: Valen Dhor Causeway
Type: location
Section: Overview

The relic engines deployed along the Valen Dhor Causeway have started attacking innocent people. Take out the misbehaving relic engines and restore peace to the Highroad!

title: Valen Dhor Causeway
text:
The relic engines deployed along the Valen Dhor Causeway have started attacking innocent people. Take out the misbehaving relic engines and restore peace to the Highroad!

Reward: Undying Storm Garment Grid, 10,000 gil.
page_content:
Title: Valen Dhor Causeway
Type: location
Section: Details

The relic engines deployed along the Valen Dhor Causeway have started attacking innocent people. Take out the misbehaving relic engines and restore peace to the Highroad!

Reward: Undying Storm Garment Grid, 10,000 gil.

title: Mount Kaelorn
text: The snow-covered, towering Mount Kaelorn is considered sacred land. Before the Endless Truce only invokers and their oathsworn were permitted to pass beyond the mountain gate as the final test of the invoker's resolve before entering the Asterreach Ruins.
page_content:
Title: Mount Kaelorn
Type: location
Section: Overview

The snow-covered, towering Mount Kaelorn is considered sacred land. Before the Endless Truce only invokers and their oathsworn were permitted to pass beyond the mountain gate as the final test of the invoker's resolve before entering the Asterreach Ruins.

title: Mount Kaelorn
text:
The Floating Ruins are situated at the peak of Mount Kaelorn. The peak used to be perpetually shrouded in fog, but after the Dreambound Scar became dormant the fog lifted revealing the ruins at the top.

Torren Kaid must hit a target with his skyball to open a new path within the caves.

Reward: Stonehewn Garment Grid, Syndicate Uniform.

Garik, in his hatred of the Sylvan Court, has decided to launch an attack! Meet him on the summit of Mount Kaelorn and stop him - by force, if that's what it takes.
page_content:

Section: Details

Reward: Stonehewn Garment Grid, Syndicate Uniform.

Garik, in his hatred of the Sylvan Court, has decided to launch an attack! Meet him on the summit of Mount Kaelorn and stop him - by force, if that's what it takes.

title: Nerys Quill
text: Nerys Quill is a slender and athletic teenage girl who has medium-length blonde hair in a high ponytail with two braids hanging down, decorated with orange feathers. Two shorter sections of hair part to her left and frame her face.
page_content:
Title: Nerys Quill
Type: character
Section: Overview

Nerys Quill is a slender and athletic teenage girl who has medium-length blonde hair in a high ponytail with two braids hanging down, decorated with orange feathers. Two shorter sections of hair part to her left and frame her face.

title: Nerys Quill
text:
Nerys Quill is a slender and athletic teenage girl who has medium-length blonde hair in a high ponytail with two braids hanging down, decorated with orange feathers. Two shorter sections of hair part to her left and frame her face. A pair of bobby pins, one blue and one green, crisscross the left side. She also has the Relicborn trademark green eyes with swirled pupils.

Nerys Quill is also one of the three characters that can fight underwater, along with Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid.

Nerys Quill is also one of the three characters that can fight underwater, along with Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid.

title: Pyrakar
text: Pyrakar is a humanoid, demonic-looking beast. He has a reddish brown complexion with light red hair, and a bulky appearance. He always appears hunchback.
page_content:
Title: Pyrakar
Type: eidolon
Section: Overview

Pyrakar is a humanoid, demonic-looking beast. He has a reddish brown complexion with light red hair, and a bulky appearance. He always appears hunchback.

title: Rhunari
text: Rhunari are humanoid in stature, standing above two meters in average height (with the males considerably larger and more muscular than the females, who possess a faster and more athletic body type). They are feline in appearance, possessing hands/claws and foot paws.
page_content:
Title: Rhunari
Type: species
Section: Overview

Rhunari are humanoid in stature, standing above two meters in average height (with the males considerably larger and more muscular than the females, who possess a faster and more athletic body type). They are feline in appearance, possessing hands/claws and foot paws.

title: Rhunari
text: Rhunari are humanoid in stature, standing above two meters in average height (with the males considerably larger and more muscular than the females, who possess a faster and more athletic body type). They are feline in appearance, possessing hands/claws and foot paws. They walk on (what would be for humans ) the balls of their feet and possess dew-claws as well as foot pads. Possessing leonine tails that roughly measure half of their body lengths, Rhunari bodies are covered in blue fur (perhaps a trait due to their living in a winter climate) with patches of white (though the color can be darker blue or yellow depending on the individual) fur around the thighs, calves, shoulders, chest (males), tail-tips, and scalp. The abundance of the fur the patches consist of seems to be greater on the males than the females. The scalp fur, or mane, is usually tied back or styled in a similar way to hair.
page_content:
Title: Rhunari
Type: species
Section: Details

Rhunari are humanoid in stature, standing above two meters in average height (with the males considerably larger and more muscular than the females, who possess a faster and more athletic body type). They are feline in appearance, possessing hands/claws and foot paws. They walk on (what would be for humans ) the balls of their feet and possess dew-claws as well as foot pads. Possessing leonine tails that roughly measure half of their body lengths, Rhunari bodies are covered in blue fur (perhaps a trait due to their living in a winter climate) with patches of white (though the color can be darker blue or yellow depending on the individual) fur around the thighs, calves, shoulders, chest (males), tail-tips, and scalp. The abundance of the fur the patches consist of seems to be greater on the males than the females. The scalp fur, or mane, is usually tied back or styled in a similar way to hair.

title: Rhunari
text:
Rhunari possess feline attributes like muzzles and fangs as well as a distinct cat-like nose. Their ears, while located similar to humans, have a more elven shape and are thought to be capable of articulation similar to cats. The male Rhunari grow a horn from the forehead that denotes status and power, and to lose it in battle is considered shameful. Kaelor was one such Rhunari who had been cast out due to its loss and his comparatively small stature next to his kin.

Both genders possess carnivore fangs, hinting that they may be meat eaters (it is unknown if they are omnivores). Their eyes are yellow, contrasting against their fur, and give the impression of a predator's gaze.

Rhunari refer to their young as "pups" despite their cat-like nature.

title: Sanctum of Ordeals
text: Aim: To descend towards the Chamber of the Dreambound in order to find Elyra Noctis.
page_content:
Title: Sanctum of Ordeals
Type: artifact
Section: Overview

Aim: To descend towards the Chamber of the Dreambound in order to find Elyra Noctis.

title: Sanctum of Ordeals
text:
Aim: To descend towards the Chamber of the Dreambound in order to find Elyra Noctis.

Examine the symbol at the back to make a glyph appear on the right wall, and touch the glyph to open the wall.

Enter the corridor and remove the Glyph Sphere from the recess, walking down the stairs afterwards.

Insert the sphere into the door below to open it, and then retrieve the sphere again once the door has stopped moving.
page_content:

Type: artifact
Section: Details

Aim: To descend towards the Chamber of the Dreambound in order to find Elyra Noctis.

Examine the symbol at the back to make a glyph appear on the right wall, and touch the glyph to open the wall.

Enter the corridor and remove the Glyph Sphere from the recess, walking down the stairs afterwards.

Insert the sphere into the door below to open it, and then retrieve the sphere again once the door has stopped moving.

title: Selene Marr
text: Selene Marr is stern and scathing (particularly to Torren Kaid), self-possessed, sardonic, moody, cynical, and does not smile often. She is nonetheless kind-hearted, caring, and intelligent and is one of the few Luminous faithful to question the religion on objective terms.
page_content:
Title: Selene Marr
Type: character
Section: Overview

Selene Marr is stern and scathing (particularly to Torren Kaid), self-possessed, sardonic, moody, cynical, and does not smile often. She is nonetheless kind-hearted, caring, and intelligent and is one of the few Luminous faithful to question the religion on objective terms.

title: Selene Marr
text: Selene Marr is stern and scathing (particularly to Torren Kaid), self-possessed, sardonic, moody, cynical, and does not smile often. She is nonetheless kind-hearted, caring, and intelligent and is one of the few Luminous faithful to question the religion on objective terms. Having lost her lover to The Hollow Eclipse, Selene Marr has become reserved and prefers to keep her emotions locked up, choosing to examine the world with realism and logic. She is a big-sister figure to Elyra Noctis, to whom she is fiercely loyal.
page_content:
Title: Selene Marr
Type: character
Section: Details

Selene Marr is stern and scathing (particularly to Torren Kaid), self-possessed, sardonic, moody, cynical, and does not smile often. She is nonetheless kind-hearted, caring, and intelligent and is one of the few Luminous faithful to question the religion on objective terms. Having lost her lover to The Hollow Eclipse, Selene Marr has become reserved and prefers to keep her emotions locked up, choosing to examine the world with realism and logic. She is a big-sister figure to Elyra Noctis, to whom she is fiercely loyal.

title: Selene Marr
text:
Selene Marr is stoic and almost never breaks her stern demeanor. Though usually uncompromising, Selene Marr is close to Torren Kaid since they grew up together and comforts him when he is upset. Selene Marr also has a seductive side to her. For example, if Caelan Veyr expresses an attraction to her, she says that she'll add Caelan Veyr to "her list" and wishes him luck. Selene Marr's personality and figure inspire awe in the group's younger girls, with Elyra Noctis confiding in her and Nerys Quill wanting to be like Selene Marr when she grows up.

Selene Marr acts as a guide during the beginning of the pilgrimage, but as her knowledge stems from the teachings of The Luminous Order, she "represents an old age bound by traditional beliefs". When the party begins to stray from the teachings, the knowledge she can impart lessens and that traditional knowledge becomes a shackle.
page_content:

title: Skyball
text: Skyball is held in open-air water sphere stadium, designed in a traditional amphitheatre style. Once the sphere is filled, a pair of triangular goals are suspended at opposing points within the sphere's diameter (usually at the north and south horizontal points).
page_content:
Title: Skyball
Type: concept
Section: Overview

Skyball is held in open-air water sphere stadium, designed in a traditional amphitheatre style. Once the sphere is filled, a pair of triangular goals are suspended at opposing points within the sphere's diameter (usually at the north and south horizontal points).

title: Skyball
text:
Skyball is held in open-air water sphere stadium, designed in a traditional amphitheatre style. Once the sphere is filled, a pair of triangular goals are suspended at opposing points within the sphere's diameter (usually at the north and south horizontal points).

By the time of the Endless Truce the skyball rules are altered slightly:

If a team leads the game by seven goals at any point, they win.

There is only one five-minute round, with no break or half time.
page_content:

title: Starfang
text: Starfang is a large, black dragon with enormous red wings. He can stand or move in either a bipedal or quadrupedal fashion.

Starfang is a large, black dragon with enormous red wings. He can stand or move in either a bipedal or quadrupedal fashion.

title: The Hollow Eclipse
text: The Hollow Eclipse has a whale-like body that it moves with a pair of clawed arms, as well as hind legs resembling pectoral fins for movement in water. It has a long tail and its body is encased in scales it can shed and turn into smaller monsters known as Eclipse shards.
page_content:
Title: The Hollow Eclipse
Type: concept
Section: Overview

The Hollow Eclipse has a whale-like body that it moves with a pair of clawed arms, as well as hind legs resembling pectoral fins for movement in water. It has a long tail and its body is encased in scales it can shed and turn into smaller monsters known as Eclipse shards.

title: The Hollow Eclipse
text:
The Hollow Eclipse has a whale-like body that it moves with a pair of clawed arms, as well as hind legs resembling pectoral fins for movement in water. It has a long tail and its body is encased in scales it can shed and turn into smaller monsters known as Eclipse shards. It carries part of a city, presumed to be a part of Asterreach, on its body close to the back of the head. The Hollow Eclipse's final form has feathery wing-like protrusions that are purple at the tips.

The Hollow Eclipse can create smaller creatures, Eclipseborn, from its outer layer. Coming in a variety of shapes and levels of power, the Eclipseborn cause trouble for any person that crosses them until The Hollow Eclipse arrives and reabsorbs them.

Gloamheart: A fusion of various Eclipseborn gathered by the Wardens of Dawn for Operation Dawnfall.
page_content:
Title: The Hollow Eclipse
Type: concept
Section: Details

It carries part of a city, presumed to be a part of Asterreach, on its body close to the back of the head. The Hollow Eclipse's final form has feathery wing-like protrusions that are purple at the tips.

The Hollow Eclipse can create smaller creatures, Eclipseborn, from its outer layer. Coming in a variety of shapes and levels of power, the Eclipseborn cause trouble for any person that crosses them until The Hollow Eclipse arrives and reabsorbs them.

Gloamheart: A fusion of various Eclipseborn gathered by the Wardens of Dawn for Operation Dawnfall.

title: The Luminous Order
text: Temples of The Luminous Order are found throughout Elyndra. At the heart of each temple lies a Chamber of the Dreambound, a room that contains a dreambound —a statue that houses a willingly-given human soul.
page_content:
Title: The Luminous Order
Type: faction
Section: Overview

Temples of The Luminous Order are found throughout Elyndra. At the heart of each temple lies a Chamber of the Dreambound, a room that contains a dreambound —a statue that houses a willingly-given human soul.

title: The Luminous Order
text:
Temples of The Luminous Order are found throughout Elyndra. At the heart of each temple lies a Chamber of the Dreambound, a room that contains a dreambound —a statue that houses a willingly-given human soul. Through the soul bonding to an invoker mighty creatures called eidolons may be made manifest.

There are five official temples of The Luminous Order, each with their own dreambound:

Veylspire – Home to the dreambound Starfang. Also the central temple of The Luminous Order.

Asterreach – At the Asterreach Dome the invoker can acquire the Crown Eidolon.
page_content:

At the heart of each temple lies a Chamber of the Dreambound, a room that contains a dreambound —a statue that houses a willingly-given human soul. There are five official temples of The Luminous Order, each with their own dreambound:

Veylspire – Home to the dreambound Starfang. Also the central temple of The Luminous Order.

Asterreach – At the Asterreach Dome the invoker can acquire the Crown Eidolon.

title: The Relicborn
text: The Relicborn have blond hair and green eyes with spiral-shaped pupils. They dress in distinctive colorful bodysuits and commonly wear goggles and masks. Despite being a marginalized group, the Relicborn have a team in the skyball league and excel at the sport.
page_content:
Title: The Relicborn
Type: faction
Section: Overview

The Relicborn have blond hair and green eyes with spiral-shaped pupils. They dress in distinctive colorful bodysuits and commonly wear goggles and masks. Despite being a marginalized group, the Relicborn have a team in the skyball league and excel at the sport.

title: The Relicborn
text:
Out of all the humanoid races in Elyndra, the Relicborn are the most expressive in their emotions and body language; many of their more notable members also exhibit personalities that may classify as eccentric or juvenile. The Relicborn take an interest in Elyndra's technological past, organizing salvage operations and excavations for ancient technology.

The Relicborn children wear full bodysuits and many The Relicborn wear glasses or goggles to hide their eyes.

The Relicborn have green eyes with spiral-shaped pupils. Regardless of skin color, all The Relicborn have blond hair (assuming they have hair). They are adept at swimming and holding their breath underwater, which serves them well during underwater excavations.

Some words have been adopted from the main Elyndran language, such as voidbeast, magic, eidolon, airship, and The Hollow Eclipse. The name of the people and their language is The Relicborn in their own language as well as in the main Elyndran language.
page_content:
Title: The Relicborn
Type: faction
Section: Details

title: The Sylvan Court
text: The Sylvan Court have a religious culture, which causes them to come across as arrogant. Their role as oathsworn of the Veilward have led to them looking down upon other races.
page_content:
Title: The Sylvan Court
Type: faction
Section: Overview

The Sylvan Court have a religious culture, which causes them to come across as arrogant. Their role as oathsworn of the Veilward have led to them looking down upon other races.

title: The Sylvan Court
text: The Sylvan Court have a religious culture, which causes them to come across as arrogant. Their role as oathsworn of the Veilward have led to them looking down upon other races. The Sylvan Court were converted to the Luminous Order religion under their leader, Vhalric, who was later appointed a High Preceptor of The Luminous Order. Despite their strict religious culture, they are generous and welcoming to guests.
page_content:
Title: The Sylvan Court
Type: faction
Section: Details

The Sylvan Court have a religious culture, which causes them to come across as arrogant. Their role as oathsworn of the Veilward have led to them looking down upon other races. The Sylvan Court were converted to the Luminous Order religion under their leader, Vhalric, who was later appointed a High Preceptor of The Luminous Order. Despite their strict religious culture, they are generous and welcoming to guests.

title: The Wardens of Dawn
text: On Veyn, the Wardens of Dawn's Lodge serves as an inn for both The Wardens of Dawn and guests to the island. Caelan Veyr is offered to rest there, where he first learns of the Wardens of Dawn's plight to defeat The Hollow Eclipse.
page_content:
Title: The Wardens of Dawn
Type: faction
Section: Overview

On Veyn, the Wardens of Dawn's Lodge serves as an inn for both The Wardens of Dawn and guests to the island. Caelan Veyr is offered to rest there, where he first learns of the Wardens of Dawn's plight to defeat The Hollow Eclipse.

title: Torren Kaid
text: Torren Kaid depends on The Luminous Order's religion and guidance, making him socially conservative and, thus, is intentionally kept in the dark about Elyra Noctis's half-The Relicborn heritage.
page_content:
Title: Torren Kaid
Type: character
Section: Overview

Torren Kaid depends on The Luminous Order's religion and guidance, making him socially conservative and, thus, is intentionally kept in the dark about Elyra Noctis's half-The Relicborn heritage.

title: Torren Kaid
text:
Torren Kaid depends on The Luminous Order's religion and guidance, making him socially conservative and, thus, is intentionally kept in the dark about Elyra Noctis's half-The Relicborn heritage. Because of his high value in his religion, Torren Kaid is reluctant to participate in anything considered forbidden by its teachings. Traveling Elyndra with Elyra Noctis's party broadens his worldview, he begins to see the hypocrisy behind his previous actions, and he apologizes to Corven and the other The Relicborn for his ignorance.

Torren Kaid has a big heart and frequently attempts to cheer the party up, though he is not always successful. He tends to goof around with the younger members of the group, much to the annoyance of Selene Marr, who berates him for setting a bad example. Though they bicker, Torren Kaid is close to her and affectionately calls her "Lu".

"Although I don't remember exactly because it was quite a long time ago—in the early stages of game development process, many songs are created based on visualization of different game scenes," music director Keiji Kawamori has explained. "At that time, I believe we decided to go with another track that was better suited for the scene. As a result, this track was ultimately not used."
page_content:
Title: Torren Kaid
Type: character
Section: Details

Torren Kaid depends on The Luminous Order's religion and guidance, making him socially conservative and, thus, is intentionally kept in the dark about Elyra Noctis's half-The Relicborn heritage. Torren Kaid has a big heart and frequently attempts to cheer the party up, though he is not always successful. He tends to goof around with the younger members of the group, much to the annoyance of Selene Marr, who berates him for setting a bad example. Though they bicker, Torren Kaid is close to her and affectionately calls her "Lu".

"Although I don't remember exactly because it was quite a long time ago—in the early stages of game development process, many songs are created based on visualization of different game scenes," music director Keiji Kawamori has explained. "At that time, I believe we decided to go with another track that was better suited for the scene. As a result, this track was ultimately not used."

title: Voltaris
text: Voltaris resembles a unicorn, with a long, golden horn and dark blue skin and gray mane and tail. He has gold bracers on his front legs.
page_content:
Title: Voltaris
Type: eidolon
Section: Overview

Voltaris resembles a unicorn, with a long, golden horn and dark blue skin and gray mane and tail. He has gold bracers on his front legs.

title: Voltaris
text: Recovered notes about Voltaris remain fragmentary, yet the surviving records still connect it to Elyndran institutions, pilgrimage-era practices, and a broader network of related entities inside the synthetic corpus.
page_content:
Title: Voltaris
Type: eidolon
Section: Details

Recovered notes about Voltaris remain fragmentary, yet the surviving records still connect it to Elyndran institutions, pilgrimage-era practices, and a broader network of related entities inside the synthetic corpus.

title: Velisara
text: Velisara has long silver hair that splays out around her in tendrils during battle. She wears a blue headband with four ribbons finished with blue beads and yellow tassels. The headdress has two long plumes that zigzag to make an 'M's shape. She has yellow eyes.
page_content:
Title: Velisara
Type: character
Section: Overview

Velisara has long silver hair that splays out around her in tendrils during battle. She wears a blue headband with four ribbons finished with blue beads and yellow tassels. The headdress has two long plumes that zigzag to make an 'M's shape. She has yellow eyes.

title: Velisara
text:
Velisara wears a blue-and-black bra with a curlicue design linked in front by a yellow chain, a black thong, a thin yellow-tasseled belt, gold bracelets, blue armbands and wristbands, green sashes decorated with the Luminous Order symbols, silver anklets, and a blue garter around her left thigh. She wears a blue bead necklace that resembles seashells or fangs. She is also barefooted.

Velisara is frank and to-the-point. While she can be kind, she can also be quite merciless, as she cares only about upholding the teachings of The Luminous Order, not hesitating to strike down those who dare to question them. She is not open to the ideas of others, viewing Elyndra as doomed without the service that she provides.

Velisara is nihilistic, believing that killing the party will free them from their pain and despair.

The party fights Velisara in the Asterreach Ruins. She has three forms, the last two of which often inflict Zombie status on the party.
